category_title: Adolescent with Down's syndrome exhibiting verbal outbursts to escape demands or gain tangibles
minimal_context: Dennis is sitting at a table in his self-contained classroom for a one-on-one session focused on writing and tracing letters.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Here is your paper. Pick up your pencil and start tracing the letters.

STUDENT: (Shakes head) No.

TUTOR: Try to trace the first letter.

STUDENT: (Puts pencil down) Stop it.

TUTOR: We need to finish writing these letters.

STUDENT: (Waves hands away) That’s enough! No!

options.A: Engage the student in drawing, painting, water coloring, and pottery making during the one-on-one session.
options.B: Provide worksheets with dotted letters on them for the student to trace during the one-on-one session.
options.C: Use large fonts with supporting pictures, reduce the number of letters to trace, provide word banks, and use speech-generating devices.
options.D: Provide modeling, role-play, and corrective feedback individually to the tutor until they reach implementation fidelity of 100%.
